tasks.direction_relation_note: The supersession docs now define the next work as replacing worktree-local planning roots with one repo-shared authority location and proving parity through a read-only shadow store before runtime authority moves.
tasks.title: Introduce canonical authority locator and shadow-store parity path
tasks.description: Resolve every active workspace to one canonical repo authority root, bootstrap the repo-shared planning store, and mirror tracked planning files into it with parity diagnostics while tracked files remain the active authority.
tasks.priority_reason: Every remaining supersession hardening slice depends on one repo-scoped authority location, so proving shadow-store parity is the first concrete step after the shipped operator loop.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Once the repo-shared shadow store exists, the next architectural step is to preserve the current `draft -> validate -> promote` UX while moving draft storage and rejection resume semantics into the authority domain.
tasks.title: Move draft, validation, and promote flow into the authority store
tasks.description: Store draft planning state, validation results, rejection archives, and resume metadata in the repo-shared authority domain so active planning remains unchanged until store-backed promote succeeds.
tasks.priority_reason: Store-backed draft semantics are the next gate after shadow-store parity because they keep the current authoring model intact while removing worktree-local draft authority.

tasks.direction_relation_note: After draft and active planning state are store-backed, hidden planner refresh and supersession claim ownership must move into the same authority domain to close split-brain and cross-process duplication risks.
tasks.title: Commit active planning refresh and queue claims in one authority domain
tasks.description: Route hidden planning refresh, queue projection rebuild, official refresh reservation, and distributor queue-head claim through one repo-scoped authority transaction model with distinct planning revision and runtime event sequencing.
tasks.priority_reason: The store does not become real supersession authority until active planning mutation and queue claims share one transactional boundary.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Once claims and active planning mutation are store-backed, the remaining hardening work is durable runtime projection plus restart recovery that reconciles external Git and GitHub truth.
tasks.title: Move runtime projections and recovery into the authority domain
tasks.description: Persist slot, session, queue, and distributor delivery projections in the repo-shared authority store, append runtime-domain events, and add restart recovery that rechecks Git, GitHub, and worktree truth before reclassifying in-flight work.
tasks.priority_reason: Cross-process claims are not sufficient without durable runtime projections and recovery that can survive restart and external side effects.

tasks.direction_relation_note: The final remaining supersession architecture step is to make the repo-shared authority store the runtime source of truth and demote tracked planning files to revision-stamped export and explicit import artifacts.
tasks.title: Cut supersession planning over to store-primary authority
tasks.description: Switch active and draft planning reads and writes to the repo-shared authority store, keep tracked planning files as revision-stamped exports, and require explicit operator import with base revision checks instead of implicit dual authority.
tasks.priority_reason: Store-primary is the final cutover after shadow-store parity, draft semantics, claims, and recovery have all been proven.
